  1. Home
  2. rule
  3. Asia
  4. Indonesia

Indonesia

How to Distinguish

  • The domain is .id
  • Cars drive on the left
  • Cigarettes are commonly sold roadside, and red and white flags are often seen in front of houses(by Geotips )
  • Many plates are black, and with blur they may look split into three blocks
  • There are many Alfamart convenience stores(References PT Sumber Alfaria Trijaya Tbk)
  • If the median is yellow, Indonesia is more likely than Malaysia

Signs you can find

By AdriansyahYS - Own work, CC BY-SA 4.0 Wikimedia Commons(Link)

    You often see red-and-white triangular flags or colorful flags lined up along streets.

    By Yan Arief Purwanto from Yogyakarta, Indonesia - IMG_9328, CC BY-SA 2.0, Link

    Tiled roofs and corrugated metal roofs are common. The farther north you go, the more metal roofs.

    Signs for Alfamart, Bank Mandiri, and BRI can appear even in small towns. Alfamart has over 17,000 stores nationwide.

    Cigarettes are commonly sold roadside. Even without a cigarette image, “18+” indicates a tobacco ad.

    Most plates are black and, with blur, can look split into three segments. In Malaysia they usually look split into two. Other colors like yellow and red also exist. If you are torn between Malaysia and Indonesia, three-way blur or front plates on motorcycles point to Indonesia.

    CC0

    CC0

    Poles often have red-and-white paint, or are black metal poles painted at the base or entirely black.

    By Fandy Aprianto Rohman - Own work, CC BY-SA 4.0, Link

    You may see PT Pertamina, Indonesia’s largest oil and gas company; the number on the sign can indicate the region(References Pertamina).

    By Yoshi Canopus - Self-photographed, CC BY-SA 3.0, Link

    Narrow two-lane roads. If the centerline is orange, consider Thailand.

    By kasman70, CC BY 3.0, Link

    There are many types, so see Indonesia bollards

    Nippon Indosari Corpindo Tbk is an Indonesian bread maker(References Official site).

    Indonesia goods

    Narrow down state/region

    • Learn where kabupaten are (see map and the reference)(References Peta Indonesia HD PDF)
      • Aceh
      • Ternate in North Maluku
      • Kupang in East Nusa Tenggara
      • Bawean
    • There are 38 provinces

      Sometimes abbreviated as “KAB”. This sign is from the Cepu area(References Cepu, Blora).

      By id:User:Mujionomaruf - Own work, CC BY-SA 4.0, Link

      There are 38 provinces. Kabupaten are a smaller unit.

      By Bennylin - Own work, derived from File:Indonesia blank map colored.svg, CC BY-SA 4.0, Link

      Architecture

      • Indonesia has many ethnic groups, so building styles vary(from 『10 More Maps You NEED To Know for Geoguessr by zi8gzag』)(References Traditional houses in Indonesia)
      • Plonkit Indonesia is detailed; use it
        • West Sumatra: Minangkabau traditional houses with curved roofs
        • North Sumatra: Batak roof styles
        • Riau: Lontiok houses with curved roofs, stilted floors, and odd-numbered stairs(References Lontiok)
        • Banten: Sulah nyanda houses of the Baduy(References Sulah nyanda)
        • Sumba: Uma Marapu houses with tall pointed roofs(References Sumba tall-roof houses)

        Tongkonan houses appear(References Toraja Tongkonan houses). They are very similar to Batak houses around Lake Toba in North Sumatra.

        Minangkabau traditional houses in West Sumatra(References Rumah Gadang). In the matrilineal Minangkabau, houses are inherited from mother to daughter.

        Batak houses in the northern highlands of Sumatra can have a roof shaped like a shallow arc.

        By Dan Lundberg - 2000 #310-19 Sumatra Samosir Island old Batak village, CC BY-SA 2.0, Link

        These are rare in cities, so they may be hard to use as GeoGuessr hints.

        By Fitri Penyalai - Own work, CC BY-SA 4.0, Link

        Lontiok houses are stilted and have curved roofs. The roof tips may have a cross(References Lontiok).

        By Hermadiyansyah Putra St Bagindo - Own work, CC BY-SA 4.0, Link

        On Java, reddish tile roofs are common.

        By monica renata from jakarta - Tarung-Waitabar, CC BY 2.0, Link

        • Agriculture varies by region
          • Rice: more likely Java (over 50%) but appears nationwide
          • Corn: if you see both rice paddies and corn fields, Java is likely
          • Oil palm: common in North Sumatra and Kalimantan
          • Data source: U.S. Indonesia Production Country Summary (USDA)

          Java is most likely (over 50%), but rice appears nationwide. If you see large paddies, consider flat central/eastern Java or southern Sulawesi.

          If you see rice paddies and corn together, go to Java.

          There are no plantations in southern Sulawesi. If you see plantations together with Jawi script, consider Riau.

          • Ternate Island has Gamalama in the center; roads and houses line the slopes
          • Area codes often appear together with place names
            • 08XX numbers are too generic; avoid hunting every sign. Place names are more useful.
            • Ignore 08 and memorize that codes increase counterclockwise starting from Jakarta.
            • 03x: East Java
            • 031x: Surabaya
            • 036x: Bali
            • 0361: Denpasar
            • 0380: Kupang
            • 07x: South Sumatra
            • 0751: Padang
            • (References Telephone numbers in Indonesia)
          • Pertamina gas station numbers can indicate region
            • 1x~5x: smaller numbers are farther north
            • 6x: Kalimantan
            • 7x: Sulawesi

            You often see the mountain in the background, and houses are built on slopes. Tidore Island next to it feels similar.

            Related Pages

            • Indonesia (wikipedia)
